message: br-ml2r.5 add centrality tie-breaks in barycenter ordering

files.patch:
@@ -418,10 +418,10 @@
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.1","title":"[FNX] Integration architecture contract and phased risk matrix","description":"Goal
Create the authoritative technical contract for integrating franken_networkx (fnx) into frankenmermaid without destabilizing current deterministic behavior.

Background
The project already has deterministic parsing/layout/rendering in Rust. fnx provides strong graph algorithms and witness artifacts but current Rust APIs are largely undirected. We need a staged plan that captures immediate wins and blocks unsafe assumptions.

Scope
- Define hard boundaries between Phase 1 (undirected structural intelligence) and Phase 2 (directed algorithm adoption).
- Define success metrics for quality, latency, determinism, and diagnostics usefulness.
- Define explicit rollback conditions and kill-switch behavior via feature flags.
- Produce implementation map across crates: fm-core, fm-parser, fm-layout, fm-cli, fm-wasm.

Acceptance Criteria
- Written architecture contract embedded in repo docs and referenced by all child beads.
- Determinism contract and fallback behavior explicitly specified.
- Risk register includes algorithm mismatch, runtime overhead, and maintenance coupling.
- Clear go/no-go gates for each integration phase.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:42:59.808188592Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:42:59.807996479Z","close_reason":"Extended FNX_INTEGRATION.md with full architecture contract: phase boundaries, success metrics, risk register (4 risks with mitigations), go/no-go gates, implementation map across crates, and evidence requirements.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fnx","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":127,"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution note: this task is the control plane for all downstream work. Do not begin adapter or diagnostics implementation without finalizing decision-contract semantics (advisory vs authoritative fnx outputs, fallback policy, and failure-mode behavior).","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:53:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.1.1","title":"[FNX] Decide dependency model and feature-flag topology","description":"Goal
Decide exactly how frankenmermaid depends on fnx (workspace path, git dependency, or published crates) with reproducible builds and minimal coupling risk.

Scope
- Evaluate dependency strategies for local development, CI, and release packaging.
- Define feature flags (`fnx-integration`, `fnx-experimental-directed`) and default states.
- Specify how to build when fnx is unavailable.

Acceptance Criteria
- Cargo dependency strategy chosen and documented with rationale.
- Feature flags implemented in Cargo manifests with deterministic defaults.
- CI matrix includes fnx-on and fnx-off paths.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.934220363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:21:05.906841526Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:21:05.906518214Z","close_reason":"Documented FNX dependency model, feature-flag topology, and offline build guidance.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","ci","fnx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.934220363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.1.2","title":"[FNX] Deterministic decision contract and fallback semantics","description":"Goal
Codify deterministic decision rules for when fnx outputs are advisory vs authoritative so behavior remains stable and explainable.

Scope
- Define precedence between existing fm-layout heuristics and fnx-derived scores.
- Define deterministic tie-break policy and stable sorting keys.
- Define fallback semantics when fnx analysis errors, times out, or is unavailable.

Acceptance Criteria
- Decision table checked into docs with executable tests.
- Tie-break and fallback paths are deterministic and traceable.
- CLI diagnostics state when fnx advice was used, skipped, or degraded.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.993681995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:05:04.968478785Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:05:04.968200328Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","diagnostics","fnx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.993681995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ml2r.10","title":"[FNX] Runtime budgets, caching, and deterministic fail-safe behavior","description":"Goal
Prevent fnx-assisted paths from causing latency spikes or runaway compute under pathological inputs.

Scope
- Add explicit analysis budgets and cancellation boundaries.
- Add cache/memoization strategy for repeated analyses.
- Add deterministic fallback behavior when budgets are exceeded.
- Emit diagnostics that explain budget-triggered fallback.

Acceptance Criteria
- Budget enforcement works in parser/layout/CLI/WASM paths.
- Fallback path preserves deterministic behavior.
- Stress corpus confirms bounded runtime behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

-{"id":"bd-ml2r.10.1","title":"[FNX] Runtime subtask: budget enforcement and cancellation semantics","description":"Goal
Implement time/iteration/work budgets for fnx analysis calls with deterministic cancellation semantics.

Scope
- Per-stage budget accounting with sane defaults.
- Deterministic timeout behavior and reason codes.
- Structured traces for budget consumption.

Acceptance Criteria
- Budget-related tests cover timeout and partial-result paths.
- No panics or hangs in adversarial scenarios.
- Diagnostics include budget exceeded reason and fallback selected.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

-{"id":"bd-ml2r.10.2","title":"[FNX] Runtime subtask: deterministic analysis cache","description":"Goal
Add deterministic cache/memoization for fnx analysis results to reduce repeated work.

Scope
- Hash-keyed cache keyed by input graph + mode + config.
- Stable invalidation strategy for config changes.
- Memory and cache-hit instrumentation.

Acceptance Criteria
- Repeat-run latency improves on representative corpora.
- Cache correctness tests prove no stale-result leakage.
- Cache behavior visible in structured logs.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

-{"id":"bd-ml2r.10.3","title":"[FNX] Runtime subtask: fallback ladder and strict-mode behavior","description":"Goal
Define and implement deterministic fallback ladder when fnx is unavailable, times out, or returns incompatible data.

Scope
- Codify fallback hierarchy and severity mapping.
- Emit clear diagnostics for fallback activation.
- Ensure user can force strict fail mode when desired.

Acceptance Criteria
- Fallback ladder is test-covered and deterministic.
- Strict mode behavior is explicit and validated.
- CLI/WASM surfaces expose fallback provenance.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.4","title":"[FNX] Cycle-removal scorer: structural penalties from fnx","description":"Goal
Improve cycle-breaking quality in Sugiyama by using fnx structural signals to avoid bad edge reversals.

Background
Current cycle removal is heuristic-heavy. Reversing structurally critical edges can produce poor readability.

Scope
- Introduce penalty model informed by articulation/bridge/cycle-basis context.
- Keep deterministic ordering and bounded runtime.
- Add side-by-side comparison harness for pathological cyclic graphs.

Acceptance Criteria
- Reduced bad reversals on curated corpus without determinism regressions.
- Runtime overhead measured and within budget.
- Fallback to existing strategy when fnx analysis unavailable.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

+{"id":"bd-ml2r.5","title":"[FNX] Ordering tie-break enrichment using centrality","description":"Goal
Use fnx centrality signals to improve rank ordering tie-breaks and reduce visual clutter.

Scope
- Compute optional centrality pre-pass (initially undirected-compatible metrics).
- Inject scores into ordering/tie-break stages in Sugiyama refinement.
- Add ablation mode to compare baseline vs fnx-assisted ordering.

Acceptance Criteria
- Ordering remains deterministic with stable score tie-break keys.
- Quality improves on dense benchmark set without unacceptable latency hit.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.642372498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T19:01:47.197782531Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T19:01:47.197172415Z","close_reason":"Integrated centrality tie-breaks into barycenter ordering with test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["centrality","fnx","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.642372498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.729649845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.795375208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:11.634328784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:11.690800326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.5.1","title":"[FNX] Ordering subtask: centrality cache and stable normalization","description":"Goal
Implement centrality cache and deterministic consumption in layout pipeline.

Scope
- Cache keyed by graph hash and projection mode.
- Inject scores into node priority map without unstable floating ordering.
- Add deterministic quantization/normalization policy.

Acceptance Criteria
- Cache hit/miss behavior is traceable.
- No nondeterministic ordering in repeated runs.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.5.2","title":"[FNX] Ordering subtask: ablation benchmark and adoption threshold","description":"Goal
Create an ablation benchmark to validate quality gain versus runtime cost for centrality-assisted ordering.

Scope
- Run baseline and fnx-assisted variants over representative corpus.
- Measure crossings, edge length variance, rank compactness, total layout ms.
- Establish adoption thresholds.

Acceptance Criteria
- Decision report clearly states when fnx centrality should be enabled by default.
- CI has deterministic benchmark-mode smoke checks.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.6","title":"[FNX] Witness and telemetry integration across parser/layout/CLI/WASM","description":"Goal
Plumb fnx complexity witnesses into frankenmermaid traces so algorithm decisions are explainable and auditable.

Scope
- Map fnx witness fields into LayoutTrace and parser diagnostics metadata.
- Add CLI JSON emission for witness artifacts.
- Ensure wasm APIs can surface witness/analysis metadata for browser tooling.

Acceptance Criteria
- Witness data is present, deterministic, and schema-documented.
- Traces clearly connect decision -> algorithm -> witness -> output quality.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
